subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF Art IV, Freedom House, RSF, EU, MONEYVAL) + international/European press.
The world's oldest republic, surrounded by Italy and using the euro; a banking-and-manufacturing economy still carrying a banking-crisis legacy (NPLs ~17%) with high-but-declining public debt.
Standout features: top-of-cohort institutions (FH 97/100) and social cohesion, against the thinnest, most Italy-dependent economy of the European microstates and a tightly-capitalized banking sector.

economic.notes: A euro-using, Italy-coupled economy with a banking-crisis legacy and high-but-declining debt.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt high but declining (~60-63% of GDP), with limited fiscal buffers and significant contingent liabilities requiring further consolidation. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: Uses the euro under an EU monetary agreement - no own monetary policy. (EU monetary agreement)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Uses the euro (a reserve currency) but issues none. (IMF)

inflation.notes: Imports eurozone inflation (~2%); no independent dynamics. (ECB; IMF)

banking_stability.notes: NPLs still ~16.9% (end-2024, from 21%), with tight capitalization at some banks - the legacy crisis is the standout risk. (IMF Art IV 2025)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Banking plus manufacturing and a tourism boom - thin and Italy-dependent; the EU Association Agreement could lift growth but adds bank competition. (IMF Art IV 2025)

institutional.notes: Top-of-cohort rule of law and an active press, with heavy defamation fines a chill risk.

rule_of_law.notes: Strong rule of law - FH 97/100, the highest of the cohort - though corruption is flagged. (Freedom House 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Credible, IMF-engaged statistics, with thin coverage. (IMF Art IV)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A competent small administration, stretched by the EU-accession and bank-restructuring workload. (IMF; EC)

press_freedom.notes: Investigative journalists active, but heavy defamation fines can encourage self-censorship; RSF does not separately rank San Marino. (Freedom House 2025)

political_social.notes: A stable, highly cohesive, homogeneous democracy (the oldest republic).

political_stability.notes: A stable parliamentary democracy (the oldest republic) - FH political rights 39/40. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A sizable Italian-resident community generally treated fairly in a small/homogeneous polity. (FH 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: Highly cohesive and homogeneous, with mild tourism-boom pressures. (FH; IMF)

demographics.notes: Small (~34k) and aging like Italy, with low fertility. (IMF)

alliance_reliability.notes: Not in NATO/EU, but surrounded by and deeply integrated with Italy (a UN/Council of Europe member), with an EU Association Agreement pending. (EC; UN)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open capital account aligned with EU/international norms; bank-sector scrutiny a residual factor. (IMF; EC)

physical_practical.notes: Low-hazard with good Italy-integrated services and very low crime.

climate.notes: Enclaved in central Italy - Mediterranean/Apennine heat, drought, and wildfire exposure, with high income aiding adaptation. (IPCC AR6; EEA)

healthcare.notes: Good universal healthcare, integrated with the Italian system for complex care. (general)

infrastructure.notes: Solid for its size, but landlocked, Italy-connected, with no airport/rail of its own. (general)

disaster_insurance.notes: Developed insurance access via Italy against moderate seismic exposure. (general)

summary: The world's oldest republic - a stable, democratic, low-crime microstate (FH 97/100, the highest of the cohort) embedded inside Italy and using the euro. Its distinguishing weakness is economic: a banking-and-manufacturing economy still carrying a banking-crisis legacy (NPLs ~17% end-2024, tightly capitalized banks), high-but-declining public debt (~60-63% of GDP), thin fiscal buffers, and heavy dependence on Italy. The EU Association Agreement would raise long-run growth but also bank compliance costs and Italian-bank competition. Strong institutions and social cohesion, but the thinnest and most financially fragile economy of the European microstates - symmetric to mildly negative skew.
